name: Medal of Valor
description: The highest law enforcement medal. Awarded for individual acts of extraordinary bravery or heroism performed in the line of duty at extreme and life-threatening personal risk.

name: Preservation of Life Medal
description: Awarded to an officer who has distinguished himself or herself by employing exceptional tactics and exercising sound judgment, beyond the normal demands of duty, to preserve the life of another.

name: Police Distinguished Service Medal
description: The department's highest award for service. Awarded to employees who distinguish themselves by performing exceptional service in a duty of great responsibility or of critical importance to law enforcement.

name: Police Commission Unit Citation
description: The department's highest unit award. Awarded to an organizational unit for bravery or outstanding service by its members functioning as a team.

name: Police Medal
description: Awarded for individual acts of heroism in the line of duty, though not above and beyond the call of duty as required for the Medal of Valor.

name: Purple Heart
description: Awarded to officers who sustain a traumatic physical injury as a result of a suspect's actions during an on-duty tactical situation. Can also be awarded posthumously.

name: Police Meritorious Service Medal
description: Awarded for performing exceptional service in a duty of great responsibility, but to a lesser degree than required for the Distinguished Service Medal.

name: Police Meritorious Achievement Medal
description: Awarded for sustained long-term noteworthy achievement or a single significant noteworthy achievement in the execution of duties.

name: Police Meritorious Unit Citation
description: Awarded to an organizational unit for bravery or outstanding service by its members functioning as a team, to a lesser degree than the Commission Unit Citation.

name: Police Star
description: Awarded for performing with exceptional judgment and/or utilizing skillful tactics in order to defuse dangerous and stressful situations.

name: Lifesaving Medal
description: Awarded for taking action in order to rescue or attempt the rescue of either a fellow officer or any person from imminent danger.

name: Police Commission Distinguished Service Medal
description: Awarded for performing exceptional service to the LAPD or performing in a stressful emergency situation with good judgment and bravery.

name: Police Commission Integrity Medal
description: Awarded for an act of exemplary integrity requiring exceptional character, fortitude, and moral courage. No longer issued.

name: Community Policing Medal
description: Awarded to personnel who have solved a significant community problem, included the community in the problem solving process, and/or shown a commitment to Community Policing.

name: Human Relations Medal
description: Awarded to officers who have shown great compassion and gone above and beyond the call of duty in their response to fellow human beings.

name: Commendation Ribbon
description: Authorized after a sworn employee has received 40 commendations. Citizen letters, minor and major commendations count.

name: Commendation Ribbon (Bronze Star)
description: Authorized after 80 commendations.

name: Commendation Ribbon (Silver Star)
description: Authorized after 120 commendations.

name: Commendation Ribbon (Gold Star)
description: Authorized after 160 commendations.

name: Reserve Exceptional Service Ribbon (Division)
description: Awarded to Reserve Officers named Reserve Officer of the Year of their Area/Division.

name: Reserve Exceptional Service Ribbon (Bureau)
description: Awarded to Reserve Officers named Reserve Officer of the Year of their Bureau.

name: Reserve Exceptional Service Ribbon (Department)
description: Awarded to Reserve Officers named Reserve Officer of the Year of the entire LAPD.

name: Patrol Service Ribbon
description: 5 years of service in patrol as a Police Officer, Sergeant and/or Lieutenant.

name: Patrol Service Ribbon (Bronze Oak Leaf)
description: 10 years of service in patrol.

name: Patrol Service Ribbon (Silver Star)
description: 15 years of service in patrol.

name: Patrol Service Ribbon (Gold Star)
description: 20 years of service in patrol.

name: Detective Service Ribbon
description: 5 years of service in investigative units as a Detective and/or Lieutenant.

name: Detective Service Ribbon (Bronze Oak Leaf)
description: 10 years of service in investigative units.

name: Detective Service Ribbon (Silver Star)
description: 15 years of service in investigative units.

name: Detective Service Ribbon (Gold Star)
description: 20 years of service in investigative units.

name: Reserve Service Ribbon
description: Awarded for 4000 hours of service as a Reserve Police officer.

name: 1984 Summer Olympics Ribbon
description: Given to any LAPD officer who saw service during the 1984 Summer Olympics from July 28 to August 12, 1984.

name: 1987 Papal Visit Ribbon
description: Given to any LAPD officer who saw service during the September 1987 pastoral visit of Pope John Paul II.

name: 1992 Civil Emergency Response Ribbon
description: Given to any LAPD officer who saw service during the 1992 Los Angeles riots from April 29 to May 4, 1992.

name: 1994 Earthquake Ribbon
description: Given to any LAPD officer who saw service during the 1994 Northridge earthquake from January 17 to January 18, 1994.

name: 2000 Democratic National Convention Ribbon
description: Given to any LAPD officer who saw service during the 2000 Democratic National Convention.

name: 2019 150th Anniversary Ribbon
description: Authorized for uniformed employees who were members of the LAPD from January 1, 2019 through December 31, 2019.

name: Urban Police Rifle Cadre Ribbon
description: Given to any LAPD officer who is qualified to use an Urban Police Rifle (AR-15 variant chambered in .223).

name: Shotgun Slug Ammunition Cadre Ribbon
description: Given to any LAPD officer who is qualified to use slug ammunition in LAPD issued shotguns.
